alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices scaling global commercial operations, transformation programs, and working with compliance/audit processes; experience with infrastructure provisioning and UAT/rollout
alignments.job_requirement: Manage and scale infrastructure, maintain systems, and support audits with technical evidence

alignments.candidate_evidence: Process design, requirements logic, automation-minded transformation experience; no production software engineering or IAM/security engineering background
alignments.job_requirement: Develop automated workflows and implement solutions to reduce attack surface and ensure compliance

alignments.candidate_evidence: Demonstrated autonomy and bias for action in commercial operations and transformation programs; no depth in security domain
alignments.job_requirement: Work autonomously in a fast-paced environment with proactive security-first mindset

gaps.gap: No production software-engineering or hands-on AWS/IAM engineering background; role requires deep hands-on AWS expertise, Terraform, Go/Python, and Linux systems knowledge
gaps.mitigation: Candidate could pursue deep AWS/IAM certifications and hands-on lab work, but this is a specialized technical foundation that typically requires years of infrastructure engineering or platform engineering experience, not operations/strategy ramp

gaps.gap: No demonstrated Access Management, PAM, JIT provisioning, or Teleport infrastructure experience
gaps.mitigation: Domain-specific expertise in access control and privileged access management is industry-specific and not easily transferable from commercial operations; would require significant hands-on mentoring and capability building

gaps.gap: Limited background in production Linux systems, networking fundamentals (OIDC, SAML, SSH, TLS, TCP/IP), and container orchestration (Kubernetes RBAC)
gaps.mitigation: These are foundational infrastructure skills typically gained through years of SRE, platform engineering, or DevOps work; unlikely to be acquired quickly through self-study alone

gaps.gap: No explicit compliance/audit translation experience in a highly regulated financial or security context
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has worked with compliance processes in commercial operations; banking compliance and security audit evidence is specialized and differs from general operations compliance

output.level_rationale: Role requires production AWS infrastructure engineering, hands-on IAM/PAM/access-control expertise, Teleport/Linux/Kubernetes systems knowledge, and Go/Python production coding. This is senior individual-contributor SRE/platform-engineering scope (L5), not strategic operations. The JD emphasizes technical depth and autonomous hands-on ownership of critical security systems, not strategy or program leadership.
output.summary: This is a hands-on Site Reliability Engineer / platform-security role requiring deep production AWS, IAM, Teleport, Terraform, Go/Python, and Linux systems expertise—fundamentally a specialized technical/engineering position (L5 SRE scope), not a strategy, operations, or transformation role. Candidate's eight-year tenure in commercial operations and transformation is strong at scope seniority, but has no production software engineering, infrastructure, security engineering, or AWS/IAM background, and the high-severity technical gaps in access management, Linux systems, networking protocols, and production coding are not manageable through operations-to-engineering transition. Recommend skip unless candidate can evidence hidden infrastructure/security engineering depth not shown in profile.
uncertainties: Company stage/size not stated in JD; N26 is a fintech but team scale is unclear
Whether candidate has any informal AWS/infrastructure/security lab experience or certifications not detailed in profile
Scope of 'Access Team' relative to broader Platform Engineering org—unclear if role could be more operations-heavy or if it is fundamentally hands-on SRE
